figures.caption: Figure 4: Total revenue for different combinations of outer loop (Out) and inner loop (In) counts for the Cohen dataset. (a) Performance under a fixed number of iterations Niters = 500. (b) Performance under a fixed computational time budget T = 30.

Figure 3: Total revenue for different combinations of outer loop (Out) and inner loop (In) counts for the Logit dataset. (a) Performance under a fixed number of iterations Niters = 500. (b) Performance under a fixed computational time budget T = 30.
Figure 2: Hypervolume convergence for Out= 15 and In= 15 under different initial stepsizes γ0, using (a) fixed iteration Niters = 100 and (b) fixed time budgets T = 600.

Figure 5: Total revenue with a fixed configuration of Out= 15 and In= 15 under different initial stepsizes for the Logit dataset, under (a) fixed iterations Niters = 500 and (b) computational time budgets T = 30.

Figure 6: Total revenue with a fixed configuration of Out= 15 and In= 15 under different initial stepsizes for the Cohen dataset, under (a) fixed iterationsNiters = 500 and (b) computational time budgets T = 30.

Figure 1: Hypervolume convergence for different outer- and inner-loop configurations. For any fixed Out, the trajectories overlap in panel (a); the same effect appears in panel (b) for Out= 15 and Out= 1.
